content.text:
Course:
International Economics and Trade (IET) — Semester 5, Unit UT5, Credits: 1, Coeff: 1
Course Objectives
Analyze international transactions (economic, commercial, monetary, and financial) and their settlement mechanisms.
Manage related risks.
Understand the global trade system and international trade policies.
Course Content
Concept, topics, and evolution of international economics.
Introduction to international trade and international finance.
Theories explaining international exchange and international trade theories.
International regional blocs.
The World Trade Organization and the new trade system.
Balance of payments.
Exchange rate systems.
Foreign exchange markets.
E-commerce.
Teaching Mode
Hybrid (20% face to face and 80% online)
Assessment Mode
Final Exam (100%)
Recommended Textbooks
Krugman, P., Obstfeld, M., & Melitz, M. International Economics: Theory and Policy, Pearson, 2018.
Alibert, R. (1978). Exchange Risk and Corporate International Finance. MacMillan, London.
Bekaert, G., & Hodrick, R. (2017). International Financial Management (3rd ed.). Cambridge University Press, UK.
Krugman, P., Obstfeld, M., & Melitz, M. (2018). International Finance (11th ed.). Pearson Education, London.
